what_it_does: Implements a client-side tutorial registry as window.MS_TUTORIALS, exposing list/get/steps accessors over a hardcoded in-memory array of tutorials and ordered steps.
observations: Defines an IIFE attached to window.MS_TUTORIALS
Hardcodes two tutorials (tut_review, tut_compose) with ordered step arrays
Each tutorial has id, title, kind, summary, surface, and steps
Each step has a target CSS selector, title, and body text
list/get/steps return shallow copies via slice()
get logs an error to the console for unknown ids and returns null
Exposes the internal T array through the _t property
No imports, dependencies, or asynchronous behavior
standouts: Tutorial content is declarative data embedded directly in source
Steps are tied to CSS selectors for UI targeting
get() is loud (console.error) on a missing tutorial
conventions: IIFE module pattern exposing a namespace on window
Comment header describes the intended API and database mirror
concerns: Hardcoded content requires code edits to add or modify tutorials
get() has a side effect (console.error) inside a query function
Exposing _t allows external mutation of internal state
No input validation or schema checks for id arguments
Steps reference CSS selectors that are not validated here
notes: Small static tutorial catalog with read-only accessors and a noisy missing-id guard.
questions: Is _t intended for debugging, testing, or external mutation?
Should tutorial data eventually be fetched from the 'tutorial'/'tutorial_step' tables mentioned in the header?
Are target selectors stable contracts with the surfaces they reference?
purpose_vs_actual: The header says it mirrors the tutorial/tutorial_step tables, but the implementation is a hardcoded in-memory array with no database access, so the durable mirror claim is aspirational rather than actual.
intention_signals: Provide a stable, easy-to-call tutorial API for the Generate workspace so tutorials can be listed and played step-by-step against specific UI targets.
novelty: Tutorial steps are pure data annotated with DOM selectors, leaving presentation logic to the caller.
contribution: A compact, self-contained tutorial data model and accessor pattern that keeps tutorial definitions declarative and ordered; useful as a seed or fallback representation of guided walkthrough content.
summary_for_embedding: A static in-browser tutorial registry exposing list, get, and steps accessors over hardcoded tutorial records with ordered CSS-targeted steps.
intention_for_embedding: To provide the Generate workspace with a step-by-step guided tutorial API backed by declarative tutorial records.

symbols.does: Returns a shallow copy of the full tutorial array.
symbols.description: list() clones the internal T array with slice() so callers receive the tutorial catalog without direct access to the mutable backing store.

symbols.does: Looks up a tutorial by id, logging an error and returning null if not found.
symbols.description: get(id) searches the internal T array for a matching tutorial id; on failure it writes a console error and returns null, making missing lookups noisy.

symbols.does: Returns a shallow copy of the steps for a given tutorial id, or an empty array if unknown.
symbols.description: steps(id) delegates to get() and, if a tutorial is found, returns a slice() of its ordered steps array; otherwise it returns an empty array.
